review_summary: Transcript is sufficient for reinforcement questions about why large-scale distributed training matters, why a very large model is a useful systems case study, and how to think about GPUs at a high level. The evidence is verbal and does not require a separate clip.

question_bank.question: What best captures why distributed training is now the default for modern deep learning?
choices: Most state-of-the-art models are still trained on a single GPU, but evaluated on many devices
Modern training commonly spans many devices, often from tens up to tens of thousands
Distributed training is only used when data augmentation fails
Large models avoid parallelism because synchronization is too costly
question_bank.explanation: Modern training jobs are usually too large for a single accelerator, so systems distribute computation across many devices working together.

transcript_quotes: But as we'll see nowadays, the new norm
on one GPU, one device, and it was

question_bank.question: Why is a 400B-scale model a useful running case study for large-scale distributed training?
choices: It is the only large model released after GPT-4
It uses no GPUs and therefore simplifies the hardware story
Its paper reveals unusually detailed training-system information compared with many frontier-model papers
It was trained entirely on one server, making distributed ideas unnecessary
question_bank.explanation: The model is useful as a teaching example because its paper shares concrete implementation and system details that many frontier-model reports omit.

transcript_quotes: we're going to be talking a lot about Llama3-405B,
and new ways of thinking in order to do that.

question_bank.question: In large-scale training, what is the most useful high-level way to think about a GPU?
choices: A graphics processor that is now widely used as a general parallel processor
A storage device optimized for checkpoint files
A network switch specialized for all-reduce traffic
A CPU feature that only accelerates integer arithmetic
question_bank.explanation: GPUs began as graphics hardware, but their massively parallel computation made them useful as general accelerators for deep learning workloads.

transcript_quotes: So first, we're going to talk a little bit about GPU hardware.
